title: 10 best herbal teas for numbness in hands

remedy_properties: Vitamin C content: Maidenhair tree tea contains high amounts of vitamin C, which helps to improve blood circulation and oxygen supply to the hands, reducing numbness caused by poor circulation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains various anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the nerves and surrounding tissues, alleviating numbness and tingling sensations.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of maidenhair tree tea help protect nerve cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness and other neuropathic symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with numbness in hands because of its ability to improve blood circulation and oxygenation to the extremities. The bioactive compounds present in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have been shown to enhance nitric oxide production, which relaxes blood vessels and promotes healthy blood flow. This increased circulation is believed to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, allowing for improved nerve function and overall well-being. Regular consumption may also enhance cognitive function.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This triterpene lactone helps with numbness in hands by improving blood circulation and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and isorhapontigenin): These polyphenolic compounds help with numbness in hands by protecting against neuronal damage and promoting the health of nerve cells, which can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.

Ginkgolide B (a sesquiterpene lactone): This constituent helps with numbness in hands by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound that can cause inflammation and damage to nerve cells, contributing to numbness and tingling sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood circulation and relieve numbness.

Seeds: The seeds are also used, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, as they contain ginkgolic acids and bilobalide, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate numbness.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are sometimes used in teas, as they are believed to contain compounds that promote blood circulation and may help relieve numbness in the hands and fingers.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove loose particles.
Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste if desired.
Consume the tea immediately and repeat as needed up to 3 times daily.

remedy_properties: Cayenne pepper tea: Contains capsaicin, which helps to increase blood flow and reduce inflammation, potentially relieving numbness in the hands.
Ginkgo biloba tea: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help improve circulation and alleviate numbness caused by poor blood supply or nerve damage.
Valerian root tea: Possesses sedative and analgesic properties, which can help reduce numbness-related discomfort, anxiety, and stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, teas helps with numbness in hands because of its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. The capsaicin present in Capsicum annuum increases blood flow and reduces pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This increased circulation and reduced pain can help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, making it a potential natural remedy for conditions like Raynaud's disease and frostbite.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin is a bioactive compound found in Capsicum annuum, which helps with numbness in hands by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, a neurotransmitter that transmits pain signals to the brain.

Dihydromyricetin (DHM): DHM is a flavonoid glycoside found in Capsicum annuum, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate numbness in hands by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide is a sesquiterpenoid found in Capsicum annuum, which may help alleviate numbness in hands by modulating the activity of certain ion channels and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for numbness due to their high content of capsaicin, which helps to increase blood flow and reduce pain.
Fruits (specifically, the ripe red peppers): They contain capsaicin, which helps to numb the pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P that transmits pain signals to the brain.
Seeds: They are also a source of capsaicin and are sometimes used to make teas for numbness, as they help to increase circulation and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 fresh capsicum annuum peppers from your garden or a local market for tea preparation.
Wash the harvested capsicum annuum peppers thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the washed capsicum annuum peppers into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep 1 teaspoon of chopped capsicum annuum in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove capsicum annuum solids.

remedy_properties: Aldosterone regulation: Chasteberry tea helps regulate aldosterone, a hormone that plays a crucial role in maintaining healthy blood flow and reducing numbness in the hands by promoting proper circulation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chaste tree tea exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with numbness in the hands.

Neuroprotective compounds: Chasteberry tea contains neuroprotective compounds like casticin and actein, which may help protect against nerve damage and promote healthy neural function, potentially alleviating numbness in the hands.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus castus, also known as chaste tree, teas helps with numbness in hands because of its potential to regulate hormonal imbalances that can cause numbness. The tea is rich in flavonoids and iridoids, which may help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the nerves. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, such as estrogen and progesterone, the tea may help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands, promoting overall nerve function and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Agnuside: A phenolic glycoside found in Vitex agnus castus, agnuside has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow.

Rotundifolin: A flavonoid glycoside, rotundifolin has antioxidant properties, which may help protect the nerves and reduce numbness in the hands by neutralizing free radicals and promoting overall nerve health.

Iridoids: A type of terpene, iridoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands by reducing inflammation, promoting blood flow, and protecting the nerves from damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used in teas due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties which may help alleviate numbness.
Seeds: Seeds are used in teas for their supposed analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may provide relief from numbness.
Flowers: The flowers are utilized in teas for their potential to reduce pain and inflammation, possibly contributing to relief from numbness in hands.
remedy_recipe: Dry the vitex agnus castus flowers in a single layer for 24 hours in a low-temperature oven at 150°f.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried vitex agnus castus flowers for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids completely.
Store the herbal tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 sprigs of foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from the garden or store-bought source.

Combine 1 teaspoon of the cut foeniculum vulgare with 8 ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and active compounds to infuse into the water.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 ounces of dried arnica montana flowers from a reputable source.
Combine the dried flowers with 8 oz of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or let it infuse for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times daily for optimal benefits.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 inner aloe vera leaves and 1 quart of water for the tea base.
Slice the aloe vera leaves into thin pieces and soak them in water for 2 hours.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl.
Boil 2 cups of water and add the aloe vera liquid to create a soothing tea.
Steep the aloe vera tea for 5-10 minutes before serving warm or chilled.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal flavor.

GABA-mimetic effect: Valerian tea contains compounds that mimic the action of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate muscle tone and relaxation. This GABA-mimetic effect can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations by calming down overactive nerves.

Antioxidant properties: Valerian tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation and free radical damage, valerian tea may help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried valeriana officinalis root or 3-4 grams of fresh root material.

Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the root material.
Discard the root and transfer the tea to a cup for consumption immediately.
Serve the valeriana officinalis tea hot or iced depending on your preference for the beverage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers just before they reach peak potency.

Strain the tea and discard the solids to create a clear liquid ready for consumption.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to numbness in hands that are treated with herbal teas are conditions such as arthritis, carpal tunnel syndrome, and peripheral neuropathy. Herbal teas like turmeric tea, ginger tea, and ginkgo biloba tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the joints and hands. Additionally, herbs like St. John's Wort and lavender have been used to alleviate nerve-related numbness and tingling sensations. These teas may be consumed on their own or in combination with other natural remedies.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for numbness in hands are a natural remedy that helps alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the fingers and palms by improving blood circulation and nerve function. The best herbal teas for numbness in hands include Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Capsicum annuum, Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Achillea millefolium, Hypericum perforatum, Lavandula angustifolia, Avena sativa, and Piper nigrum. If left untreated, numbness in hands can significantly impact daily life, causing difficulties in performing tasks, loss of dexterity, and reduced productivity, ultimately affecting one's ability to work, drive, or even care for themselves.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with numbness in hands is a blend of Ginger, Turmeric, and Ginkgo Biloba. Ginger tea helps to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, while Turmeric tea's anti-inflammatory properties aid in relieving numbness and pain. Ginkgo Biloba tea, rich in antioxidants, promotes blood flow to the hands, helping to regain sensation. Together, these teas provide a natural and effective solution to alleviate numbness and promote overall hand health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with numbness in hands because it improves blood circulation to the extremities. remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Heat the water to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit in a saucepan.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the leaves and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature for 10 minutes.
